items.title: Porepunkah property owner makes new claims about Dezi Freeman incident

children.content: The owner of the Porepunkah property where Dezi Freeman shot two police officers says he rang and spoke to Freeman soon after the shooting.

gallery.altText: A man stands against a rural property

items.title: Taylor vows to alter Sex Discrimination Act following 'Tickle v Giggle' ruling

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has vowed to amend the Sex Discrimination Act after the Federal Court upheld a landmark ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app was discriminatory.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: After her husband's death, Annie discovered he had placed 100 bets a day

children.content: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.

image.altText: A close up of a woman starring off to the left with a serious look on her face

items.title: Former UK health minister says he will stand in any contest to replace Starmer as PM

children.content: Former health secretary Wes Streeting, who resigned from cabinet on Thursday in preparation for the challenge, says his party needs a "proper contest" with the best candidates on the field.

image.altText: A young man speaks passionately at a conference.

items.title: Top Hamas military leader killed in Israeli air strikes, official confirms

children.content: Izz al-Din al-Haddad is the most senior Hamas official to be killed by Israel since a US-backed ceasefire agreement meant to halt fighting was implemented in October.

image.altText: The shell of a car on fire with people crowding around.

items.title: Labor on track to win by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford

children.content: At the end of counting on Saturday night, there has been a 4.1 per cent swing against Labor after preferences, with additional postal votes still to be counted.

articles.title: See full results from the Stafford by-election

items.title: Friends witness 'horrific' shark attack that claimed life of spearfishing mate

children.content: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.

image.altText: Police and ambulance parked near a jetty at a bay

items.title: Eight dead and 35 injured after train collides with public bus in Bangkok

children.content: Footage of the incident shows a group of vehicles queuing across a railway line, before a cargo train suddenly ploughs into the side of the bus and pushes it into oncoming traffic.

image.altText: A still from CCTV footage shows a train pushing a flaming bus through a crowd of cars.

items.title: Melbourne City claims ALW championship as Matildas stars shine against Phoenix

children.content: Sensational strikes from young stars Holly McNamara and Leticia McKenna have helped Melbourne City to a record-equalling fifth A-League Women championship.

image.altText: A group of soccer players in light blue huddle and jump in the air in celebration

items.title: Penalty agony for Newcastle as Sydney FC books place in ALM grand final

children.content: Eli Adams's 96th-minute equaliser proves to be in vain as Sydney FC downs Newcastle on penalties to progress to the A-League Men season decider.

image.altText: A group of soccer players in white run towards the camera, smiling and with arms outstretched

children.content: US President Donald Trump says American and Nigerian forces have killed the second-in-command leader of ISIS.

image.altText: President Donald Trump talks to reporters before he boards Air Force One on Saturday.

items.title: Who was still watching Harvey Weinstein almost a decade on from #MeToo?

children.content: Almost a decade after the #MeToo movement began, disgraced producer Harvey Weinstein's latest trial took place in a new climate. But supporters says its significance remains.

image.altText: A close up of Harvey Weinstein's face.

children.content: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.

items.title: Origin hopefuls stand up as Cowboys coach hails 'gritty' win

children.content: Todd Payten said his side's victory over the Roosters was "as good a win as I've had" with the club as Melbourne notched back-to-back wins after a messy 34-8 victory over Parramatta and the Sea Eagles thrash Wests Tigers in Magic Round at Lang Park.

items.title: Blues claim spirited victory over Dogs to begin post-Voss life in style

children.content: Carlton beats the Western Bulldogs by 12 points in its first game since Michael Voss left the club, while Melbourne upsets Hawthorn in style. Follow live.

image.altText: Carlton players converge around Jack Ison in celebration of a goal

items.title: A hearty slow-cooked beef stew that will warm you up as the weather cools

children.content: Just 15 minutes of prep means your slow cooker will have a rich beef stew waiting at dinner time.

items.title: A little box of joy came into my life at just the right time

children.content: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a 90s club banger

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: A Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something much bigger

children.content: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

items.title: Is the US-China rivalry over?

children.content: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

items.title: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools

children.content: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

image.altText: Jane Caro

items.title: Tasmania to close legal loophole to help child sex abuse survivors

children.content: Tasmania will introduce laws to close a loophole which allows some organisations to avoid being liable for abuse perpetrated by individuals who aren't formal employees.

image.altText: Man in black suit stands for portrait in park in Autumn

items.title: Boy dead, two injured in car crash in north-west Victoria

children.content: Police say a car stolen from Mildura last night was travelling along the Calder Highway when it veered off the road and struck a tree.

image.altText: A car crashed against a tree on the side of a highway with debris strewn near the car.

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

gallery.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

thumbnail.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: Amid grief, the NT's CLP rushes drastic child protection changes

children.content: Stakeholders have just one week to respond to sweeping changes to NT child protection laws that Aboriginal groups say could do "generations" of harm.

image.altText: A woman writes on a poster by candlelight

items.title: 'Destroys trust in the system': Rally held for Isla Bell

children.content: A rally has been held for Isla Bell in Melbourne after friends and family were left devastated when murder and manslaughter charges were dropped against the man accused of killing the 19-year-old.

image.altText: Hands holding a photo of Isla Bell

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians unlikely to be paid out, experts say

children.content: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.

items.title: Scottish ref under police protection as Celtic penalty sparks threats

children.content: Scottish FA condemns "vigilantism" after a referee's personal details are leaked online following a highly controversial penalty in the Scottish Premier League.

image.altText: A Scottish football referee runs back from the sideline pointing towards the penalty spot as players watch.

items.title: Kohli to play 2027 World Cup only if he can add value

children.content: Virat Kohli says if he plays in the 2027 ICC World Cup he will compete for "every ball like it's the last ball" - but vows if he can't add value to India's team he will not be there.

image.altText: Indian cricket star Virat Kohli shakes hands as he walks off following an ODI win, with Australian players in the background.

items.title: Stolen car involved in alleged shooting spotted at SA prison staff car park days earlier

children.content: The Public Service Association is calling for urgent and immediate security upgrades after a stolen car allegedly involved in a shooting was seen at the Yatala Labour Prison in the days before the incident.

image.altText: Nissan SUV

items.title: 'The sword is still hanging': Farmers in limbo as Inland Rail land sits idle

children.content: For more than a decade, Australians in the path of the proposed Inland Rail corridor have wondered if the nation-building project will force them from their homes. Although the northern section has been scrapped, the legacy of its blueprints is still fuelling anxiety.

image.altText: a man with hand on hip stands in front of his brown soil

items.title: Frustrated Scheffler slams 'absurd' pin positions after disappointing round
